summary:
The claim asserts that only one option among those provided fails to pierce the buccinator muscle. Agent1 and Agent2 concur that the correct choice is D because the parotid duct (C), which opens adjacent to the buccinator without piercing it, distinguishes itself from other options like the labial branch of the facial nerve (A). This summary maintains clarity about why each agent supports their respective answers, emphasizing the critical anatomical fact regarding the parotid duct’s non-penetrating relationship with the buccinator.
